Column_3:
1) Asked about Python scripting then ECE related questions, after I mentioned I am from ece
2) There were 2 technical interviews. 
First interview: 
-Project based questions, i used authentication and sockets in my project so a lot of questions were asked on that.
-they asked if i knew linux and since i only had a basic idea about it they skipped asking linux questions
- For about 30 minutes I was only asked networking questions like OSI model, difference between TCP and UDP, HTTP, what is the type of encryption you have used in your project, Explain all the protocols you know eg FTP, POP, HTTP, IP etc, DHCP, NAT, DNS, why is DNS used etc.
- I was also asked to explain how cookies work
Second interview: 
-The interviewer had opened my github and was asking about all the projects in my github.
-He told me to explain my project and even he asked me about encryption.
- I had used docker in my project so questions were asked on that.
-Since i used django channels for using Sockets, he asked what was the use of redis, what is the schema of redis and how am i using it.
- Another one of my projects used google maps api, so i was asked what is latitude and longitude, what determines time zone, explain GPS, min number of satellites required for gps to work etc
-Then since my github had a lot of python projects and codes, he asked me why do i use anaconda, few questions on jupyter were asked. 
- As i used Django for my projects he asked few questions on django, like what port dies django use, is the protocol used http or https, what is the difference between http and https.

Column_2:
There are total 3 rounds in the hiring process. 
The first round consists of two sections-Cognitive & Technical Assessment and Coding Round. 
There were a mix of questions from Cognitive Section which includes English Ability, Critical Reasoning & Problem Solving, Abstract Reasoning and from Technical Section which includes Common Application & MS Office, Pseudocode, Network Security & Cloud. There were 90 questions (Cognitive Section-50 questions and Technical Section-40 questions) to be solved in 90 minutes. This is an elimination round and only after you clear this round you'll proceed to the coding round. 
There were 2 questions to be solved in 45 minutes and you are free to choose any programming language of your choice. Both the sections are attempted one after the other on the same day. 
Second round is Communication Assessment which consists of questions from Reading (8 questions), Listening/Repeat (16 questions), Q&A (24 questions), Sentence Jumble/Sentence Builds (10 questions), Story Telling (3 questions), Speaking (2 questions). There were 63 questions to be solved in approximately 30 minutes. Each question in each section is timed. This is not an elimination round but is mandatory to attempt.
The third round was basically an HR Interview Round. It lasts for about 20 minutes. Some of the questions asked by the interviewer are listed below:
1. Introduce yourself
2. Tell me about your projects in brief
3. Tell me some of the problems faced during projects
4. How did you handle any conflicts during projects
5. How many members were there in your team and what was your role in the project
6. Tell me about any one of your achievements that you are proud of 
7. What are your strengths and weaknesses
8. Do you have any certifications
9. What is your most favourite and least favourite subject
10. Do I have any questions for them
